post.summary:
POST /api/v1/build
Start a new build from multipart upload (Dockerfile + context tarball)
post.description:
Accepts a multipart form with:
- `dockerfile`: The Dockerfile content (optional if using runtime)
- `context`: A tarball containing the build context
- `config`: JSON configuration (`BuildRequest`)

# Errors

Returns an error if the request is invalid, context extraction fails, or the build
cannot be started.

post.summary:
POST /api/v1/build/json
Start a new build from JSON request with a context path on the server.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the context path is invalid or the build cannot be started.

get.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the build is not found.

# Errors

Returns an error if the build is not found or logs cannot be read.

get.summary:
GET /api/v1/build/{id}/stream
Stream build progress via Server-Sent Events.
get.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the build is not found or not active.

# Errors

Returns an error if the user is not authenticated.

Returns all containers managed through this API. Optionally filter by
label using the `label` query parameter in `key=value` format.

# Errors

Returns an error if the user is not authenticated.

post.description:
Pulls the image if needed, creates the container, and starts it.
Returns the container info including its assigned ID.

# Errors

Returns an error if image pull fails, container creation fails, or the
user lacks the operator role.

get.summary: Get details for a specific container.
get.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the container is not found or the user is not authenticated.

delete.description:
Sends a stop signal (with a 30-second timeout), then removes the container.

# Errors

Returns an error if the container is not found, stop/remove fails, or the
user lacks the operator role.

post.summary: Execute a command in a running container.
post.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the container is not found, the command is invalid,
execution fails, or the user lacks the operator role.

get.description:
Returns the last N lines of container logs as plain text, or streams
logs as Server-Sent Events when `follow=true`.

# Errors

Returns an error if the container is not found or log retrieval fails.

parameters.name: tail
parameters.description: Number of tail lines to return

200.description: Container logs (plain text or SSE stream)

get.description:
Returns CPU and memory usage statistics for the specified container.

# Errors

Returns an error if the container is not found or stats retrieval fails.

get.summary: Wait for a container to exit and return its exit code.
get.description:
This endpoint blocks until the container exits. Useful for CI runners
that need to wait for a build/test container to complete.

# Errors

Returns an error if the container is not found or the wait fails.

# Errors

Returns an error if storage access fails.

post.description:
When the daemon has orchestration wired (service manager, proxy, overlay),
this handler:
 1. Parses and validates the spec YAML
 2. Stores the deployment with status `Deploying`
 3. Spawns an async task that registers services, sets up overlays,
    configures the proxy, and scales services
 4. Returns immediately with `Deploying` status
 5. The async task updates the stored status to `Running` or `Failed`

Without orchestration wired, it stores the spec with `Pending` status.

# Errors

Returns an error if the spec is invalid or storage fails.

get.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the deployment is not found or storage access fails.

# Errors

Returns an error if the deployment or service is not found.

Returns service logs as plain text when `follow=false`, or as a Server-Sent
Events stream when `follow=true`.  In follow mode the server first emits the
last N lines (controlled by the `lines` parameter) and then continuously
polls for new output, emitting each new line as a `data:` SSE event.

# Errors

Returns an error if the deployment or service is not found, or the service
manager is not available.

200.description: Service logs (plain text or SSE stream)

post.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the deployment or service is not found, scaling fails,
or the user lacks permission.

get.summary: Get deployment details (with live per-service health when available).
get.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the deployment is not found or storage access fails.

delete.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if the deployment is not found, storage access fails, or teardown
encounters critical failures.

get.summary: Get the current replica count for a service.
get.description:
This endpoint allows the scheduler to query the current state of a service.

# Errors

Returns an error if the service is not found or authentication is invalid.

401.description: Unauthorized - invalid or missing internal token

post.summary: Scale a service via internal scheduler request.
post.description:
This endpoint is called by the distributed scheduler leader to trigger
scaling operations on agent nodes. It uses a shared secret for authentication.

# Errors

Returns an error if the service is not found, scaling fails, or authentication
is invalid.

401.description: Unauthorized - invalid or missing internal token

get.summary: List all secrets for the authenticated user.
get.description:
Returns metadata for all secrets in the user's scope. Secret values are
never exposed through this endpoint.

# Errors

Returns an error if storage access fails.

post.description:
Stores a new secret or updates an existing one. The secret value is encrypted
at rest and the version number is incremented on updates.

# Errors

Returns an error if validation fails, storage operations fail, or the user
lacks permission.

get.summary: Get metadata for a specific secret.
get.description:
Returns metadata for a single secret. The secret value is never exposed
through this endpoint.

# Errors

Returns an error if the secret is not found or storage access fails.

delete.description:
Permanently removes a secret from the store.

# Errors

Returns an error if the secret is not found, storage access fails, or
the user lacks permission.

post.description:
# Errors

Returns an error if credentials are invalid or token creation fails.

runtime.description: Use runtime template instead of Dockerfile

tags.description: Tags to apply to the image

BuildRequestWithContext.description: Build request with server-side context path

context_path.description: Path to the build context on the server

runtime.description: Use runtime template instead of Dockerfile

ContainerExecRequest.description: Exec request for running a command in a container

ContainerInfo.description: Container information returned by the API

memory_limit.description: Memory limit in bytes (`u64::MAX` if unlimited)

memory_percent.description: Memory usage as percentage of limit

ContainerWaitResponse.description: Wait response with container exit code

CreateContainerRequest.description: Request to create and start a container

CreateSecretRequest.description: Request to create or update a secret

value.description: The secret value (will be encrypted at rest)

DeploymentDetails.description: Deployment details (enhanced with per-service health and endpoints)

SecretMetadataResponse.description: Response containing secret metadata (never the value)

created_at.description: Unix timestamp when the secret was created

updated_at.description: Unix timestamp when the secret was last updated

version.description: Version number of the secret (incremented on each update)

ServiceHealthInfo.description: Per-service health info included in deployment details

detect_files.description: Files that indicate this runtime should be used
